rules: We're testing the product, not you. There are no wrong answers.
Please think out loud — tell me what you're looking at and thinking.
If you get stuck, tell me what you expected to happen.
setup.noHelpPolicy: Give 10 seconds of silence before prompting. Ask: 'What would you do next if I wasn't here?'

warmup.intro: Ask these BEFORE showing the prototype.
warmup.why: Understand their current mental model before introducing the product.

questions.label: Current process
questions.question: How do you currently do [this task]?
questions.listenFor: What tools do they use? What's painful?

questions.label: Recent example
questions.question: Tell me about the last time you did [this task]. What happened?

tasks.name: First impression
tasks.subtitle: Exploration without clicking
tasks.script: Look at this screen for a minute. Without clicking, tell me what you think this is for and what you would do first.
tasks.success: Can articulate the purpose. Points to a reasonable starting point.
tasks.learnWhy: Tests basic comprehension before any interaction.

tasks.name: Core task
tasks.subtitle: Main user flow
tasks.script: Now try to [complete the main task]. Talk me through what you're doing.
tasks.success: Completes the task without major errors or dead ends.
watchFor: Do they find the key feature?

tasks.name: Edge case
tasks.subtitle: Secondary flow
tasks.script: Now imagine [edge case scenario]. How would you handle that?
tasks.success: Finds a reasonable path or explains what they'd expect.
watchFor: Do they discover the secondary feature?
tasks.learnWhy: Tests whether edge cases are handled.

wrapup.intro: Ask after all tasks are complete.

questions.label: Overall
questions.question: What worked well? What was frustrating?

questions.label: Missing
questions.question: Was anything missing that you expected to see?

questions.label: Trust
questions.question: Would you trust this tool for [the task]? Why or why not?

observerNotes.meaning: Mental model mismatch — note what they expected
